html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eighteen — Part Five

The Book of Yirmeyahu

YIRMEYAHU

The Book of Jeremiah — DSS Shorter Recension Primary

Chapters 32 through 39

Yirmeyahu Buys a Field — The Rekhabites — The Burned Scroll — The Fall of Yerushalayim — Eved-Melekh the Kushite

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJer-a and 4QJer-b — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTERS 32 THROUGH 39 — THE SIEGE AND THE FALL OF YERUSHALAYIM

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 32 Yirmeyahu Buys a Field — The Siege Is On — Houses and Fields Shall Again Be Bought — Nothing Is Too Hard for YHWH

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH in the tenth year of Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah, which was the eighteenth year of Nevukhadnetzar.

2 At that time the army of the king of Babylon was besieging Yerushalayim, and Yirmeyahu the prophet was shut up in the court of the guard that was in the palace of the king of Yehudah.

3 For Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah had imprisoned him, saying, Why do you prophesy and say, Thus says YHWH: Behold, I am giving this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall capture it;

4 Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah shall not escape out of the hand of the Chaldeans, but shall surely be given into the hand of the king of Babylon, and shall speak with him face to face and see him eye to eye.

5 And he shall take Tzidkiyahu to Babylon, and there he shall remain until I visit him, declares YHWH. Though you fight against the Chaldeans, you shall not succeed?

6 Yirmeyahu said, The word of YHWH came to me:

7 Behold, Khanamel the son of Shallum your uncle will come to you and say, Buy my field that is at Anatot, for the right of redemption by purchase is yours.

8 Then Khanamel my cousin came to me in the court of the guard, in accordance with the word of YHWH, and said to me, Buy my field that is at Anatot in the land of Binyamin, for the right of possession and redemption is yours. Buy it for yourself. Then I knew that this was the word of YHWH.

9 And I bought the field at Anatot from Khanamel my cousin, and weighed out the money to him, seventeen shekels of silver.

10 I signed the deed, sealed it, got witnesses, and weighed the money on scales.

11 Then I took the sealed deed of purchase, containing the terms and conditions, and the open copy.

12 And I gave the deed of purchase to Barukh the son of Neriyahu son of Makhseiyahu, in the presence of Khanamel my cousin, in the presence of the witnesses who signed the deed of purchase, and in the presence of all the Yehudim who were sitting in the court of the guard.

13 I charged Barukh in their presence, saying,

14 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Take these deeds, both this sealed deed of purchase and this open deed, and put them in an earthenware vessel, that they may last for a long time.

15 For thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Houses and fields and vineyards shall again be bought in this land.

16 After I had given the deed of purchase to Barukh the son of Neriyahu, I prayed to YHWH, saying:

17 Ah, YHWH my Creator — it is you who have made the heavens and the earth by your great power and by your outstretched arm. Nothing is too hard for you.

18 You show steadfast love to thousands, but you repay the guilt of fathers to their children after them, O great and mighty Creator, whose name is YHWH of hosts,

19 Great in counsel and mighty in deed, whose eyes are open to all the ways of the children of man, rewarding each one according to his ways and according to the fruit of his deeds.

20 You have shown signs and wonders in the land of Egypt, and to this day in Yisra'EL and among all mankind, and have made a name for yourself, as at this day.

21 You brought your people Yisra'EL out of the land of Egypt with signs and wonders, with a strong hand and outstretched arm, and with great terror.

22 And you gave them this land, which you swore to their fathers to give them, a land flowing with milk and honey.

23 And they entered and took possession of it. But they did not obey your voice or walk in your Torah. They did nothing of all you commanded them to do. Therefore you have made all this disaster come upon them.

24 Behold, the siege mounds have come up to the city to take it, and because of sword and famine and pestilence the city is given into the hands of the Chaldeans who are fighting against it. What you spoke has come to pass, and behold, you see it.

25 Yet you, O YHWH my Creator, have said to me, Buy the field for money and get witnesses — though the city is given into the hands of the Chaldeans.

26 The word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu:

27 Behold, I am YHWH, the Creator of all flesh. Is anything too hard for me?

28 Therefore, thus says YHWH: Behold, I am giving this city into the hands of the Chaldeans and into the hand of Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, and he shall capture it.

29 The Chaldeans who are fighting against this city shall come and set this city on fire and burn it, with the houses on whose roofs offerings have been made to Ba'al and drink offerings have been poured out to other gods, to provoke me to anger.

30 For the children of Yisra'EL and the children of Yehudah have done nothing but evil in my sight from their youth. The children of Yisra'EL have done nothing but provoke me to anger by the work of their hands, declares YHWH.

31 This city has aroused my anger and wrath, from the day it was built to this day, so that I will remove it from my sight

32 Because of all the evil of the children of Yisra'EL and the children of Yehudah that they did to provoke me to anger — their kings and their officials, their priests and their prophets, the men of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.

33 They have turned their back to me and not their face. And though I have taught them persistently, they have not listened to receive instruction.

34 They set up their abominations in the house that is called by my name, to defile it.

35 They built the high places of Ba'al in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom, to offer up their sons and daughters to Molekh, though I did not command them, nor did it enter into my mind, that they should do this abomination, to cause Yehudah to sin.

36 Now therefore thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, concerning this city of which you say, It is given into the hand of the king of Babylon by sword, by famine, and by pestilence:

37 Behold, I will gather them from all the countries to which I drove them in my anger and my wrath and in great indignation. I will bring them back to this place, and I will make them dwell in safety.

38 And they shall be my people, and I will be their Creator.

39 I will give them one heart and one way, that they may fear me forever, for their own good and the good of their children after them.

40 I will make with them an everlasting covenant, that I will not turn away from doing good to them. And I will put the fear of me in their hearts, that they may not turn from me.

41 I will rejoice in doing them good, and I will plant them in this land in faithfulness, with all my heart and all my soul.

42 For thus says YHWH: Just as I have brought all this great disaster upon this people, so I will bring upon them all the good that I promise them.

43 Fields shall be bought in this land of which you are saying, It is a desolation, without man or beast — it is given into the hand of the Chaldeans.

44 Fields shall be bought for money, and deeds shall be signed and sealed and witnessed, in the land of Binyamin, in the places around Yerushalayim, and in the cities of Yehudah, in the cities of the hill country, in the cities of the Shephelah, and in the cities of the Negev. For I will restore their fortunes, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 32 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The field purchase at Anatot — while the city is under siege — is the covenant sign-act of restoration: buying property in a land the covenant community is about to be expelled from is the covenant declaration that YHWH's restoration purpose will outlast the Babylonian occupation. The deed is sealed in a jar to last a long time — verse 14.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 32 in full. Verse 17 — nothing is too hard for you — and verse 27 — is anything too hard for me? — form the covenant theological bracket of the chapter. Yirmeyahu's prayer confesses YHWH's power; YHWH's response confirms it. The sign-act of land purchase only makes covenant sense if YHWH's power to restore is real.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 32. Verses 39-41 — I will give them one heart and one way — I will make an everlasting covenant — I will rejoice in doing them good with all my heart and all my soul — is the covenant restoration promise that extends and deepens the new covenant of Chapter 31. YHWH commits not just covenant Torah-inscription but covenant joy — I will rejoice in doing them good.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — houses and fields and vineyards shall again be bought in this land. The covenant prophetic sign-act compresses the entire covenant restoration into a commercial transaction. While the city burns and the covenant community is being marched to Babylon, Yirmeyahu signs the deed, seals it, weighs the silver, and files it in a jar. The covenant future is stated not in poetry but in property law. The sealed deed is YHWH's covenant mortgage on the land: it will be redeemed.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 33 Call to Me and I Will Answer — I Will Restore the Fortunes of Yehudah — The Branch of Righteousness — The Covenant With Day and Night

1 The word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu a second time, while he was still shut up in the court of the guard:

2 Thus says YHWH who made the earth, YHWH who formed it to establish it — YHWH is his name:

3 Call to me and I will answer you, and will tell you great and hidden things that you have not known.

4 For thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, concerning the houses of this city and the houses of the kings of Yehudah that were torn down to make a defense against the siege mounds and against the sword:

5 They are coming in to fight against the Chaldeans and to fill them with the dead bodies of men whom I shall strike down in my anger and my wrath, for I have hidden my face from this city because of all their evil.

6 Behold, I will bring to it health and healing, and I will heal them and reveal to them abundance of prosperity and security.

7 I will restore the fortunes of Yehudah and the fortunes of Yisra'EL, and rebuild them as they were at first.

8 I will cleanse them from all the guilt of their sin against me, and I will forgive all the guilt of their sin and rebellion against me.

9 And this city shall be to me a name of joy, a praise and a glory before all the nations of the earth who shall hear of all the good that I do for them. They shall fear and tremble because of all the good and all the prosperity I provide for it.

10 Thus says YHWH: In this place of which you say, It is a waste without man or beast, in the cities of Yehudah and the streets of Yerushalayim that are desolate, without man or inhabitant or beast, there shall be heard again

11 The voice of mirth and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the voices of those who sing, as they bring thank offerings to the house of YHWH: Give thanks to YHWH of hosts, for YHWH is good, for his steadfast love endures forever. For I will restore the fortunes of the land as at first, says YHWH.

12 Thus says YHWH of hosts: In this place that is waste, without man or beast, and in all of its cities, there shall again be habitations of shepherds resting their flocks.

13 In the cities of the hill country, in the cities of the Shephelah, and in the cities of the Negev, in the land of Binyamin, the places about Yerushalayim, and in the cities of Yehudah, flocks shall again pass under the hands of the one who counts them, says YHWH.

14 Behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will fulfill the promise I made to the house of Yisra'EL and the house of Yehudah.

15 In those days and at that time I will cause a righteous Branch to spring up for Dawid, and he shall execute justice and righteousness in the land.

16 In those days Yehudah will be saved, and Yerushalayim will dwell securely. And this is the name by which it will be called: YHWH Is Our Righteousness.

17 For thus says YHWH: Dawid shall never lack a man to sit on the throne of the house of Yisra'EL,

18 And the Levitical priests shall never lack a man in my presence to offer burnt offerings, to burn grain offerings, and to make sacrifices forever.

19 The word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu:

20 Thus says YHWH: If you can break my covenant with the day and my covenant with the night, so that day and night will not come at their appointed time,

21 Then also my covenant with Dawid my servant may be broken, so that he shall not have a son to reign on his throne, and my covenant with the Levitical priests my ministers.

22 As the host of heaven cannot be numbered and the sands of the sea cannot be measured, so I will multiply the offspring of Dawid my servant, and the Levitical priests who minister to me.

23 The word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu:

24 Have you not observed that these people are saying, YHWH has rejected the two clans that he chose? Thus they have despised my people so that they are no longer a nation in their sight.

25 Thus says YHWH: If I have not established my covenant with day and night and the fixed order of heaven and earth,

26 Then I will reject the offspring of Yaakov and Dawid my servant and will not choose one of his offspring to rule over the offspring of Avraham, Yitzkhak, and Yaakov. For I will restore their fortunes and will have mercy on them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 33 with some notable differences from the Masoretic. Verses 14-26 are absent from the Septuagint, suggesting they may be a Masoretic expansion not present in the earlier text. The DSS evidence is complex here. The covenant of day and night — verses 20-26 — grounds the Davidic covenant's permanence in the created order: as long as day and night continue, YHWH's covenant with Dawid stands.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 33 in full. Verse 3 — call to me and I will answer you, and will tell you great and hidden things that you have not known — is read as the covenant invitation to prophetic prayer: the covenant prophet in prison receives the covenant promise of divine revelation precisely in his most constrained moment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves Chapter 33 in its longer form including the Davidic covenant permanence oracle of verses 14-26. Verse 8 — I will cleanse them from all the guilt of their sin against me, and I will forgive all the guilt of their sin and rebellion — is the covenant promise of total covenant forgiveness that corresponds to Chapter 31:34 — I will forgive their iniquity and remember their sin no more.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20-21 — if you can break my covenant with the day and my covenant with the night, so that day and night will not come at their appointed time, then also my covenant with Dawid may be broken. The covenant permanence of the Davidic promise is grounded in creation order: the regularity of day and night is YHWH's covenant signature on the created world. The people saying YHWH has rejected the two chosen clans — verse 24 — are contradicted by the evidence of every sunrise.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 34 The Word to Tzidkiyahu — You Shall Die in Peace — The Covenant of Freedom Broken — They Profaned My Name

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH, when Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon and all his army and all the kingdoms of the earth under his dominion and all the peoples were fighting against Yerushalayim and all of its cities:

2 Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Go and speak to Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah and say to him, Thus says YHWH: Behold, I am giving this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall burn it with fire.

3 You shall not escape from his hand but shall surely be captured and delivered into his hand. You shall see the king of Babylon eye to eye and speak with him face to face. And you shall go to Babylon.

4 Yet hear the word of YHWH, O Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah. Thus says YHWH concerning you: You shall not die by the sword.

5 You shall die in peace. And as spices were burned for your fathers, the former kings who were before you, so people shall burn spices for you and lament for you, saying, Ah, lord — for I have spoken the word, declares YHWH.

6 Then Yirmeyahu the prophet spoke all these words to Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah, in Yerushalayim,

7 When the army of the king of Babylon was fighting against Yerushalayim and against all the cities of Yehudah that were left, Lakhish and Azekah, for these were the only fortified cities of Yehudah that remained.

8 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH, after King Tzidkiyahu had made a covenant with all the people in Yerushalayim to make a proclamation of liberty to them,

9 That everyone should set free his Hebrew slaves, male and female, so that no one should enslave a Yehudi, his brother.

10 And they obeyed, all the officials and all the people who had entered into the covenant that everyone would set free his slave, male or female, so that they would not be enslaved again. They obeyed and set them free.

11 But afterward they turned around and took back the male and female slaves they had set free, and brought them into subjection as slaves.

12 The word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH:

13 Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: I myself made a covenant with your fathers when I brought them out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery, saying,

14 At the end of seven years each of you must set free the fellow Hebrew who has been sold to you and has served you six years. You must set him free from your service. But your fathers did not listen to me or incline their ears to me.

15 You recently repented and did what was right in my eyes by proclaiming liberty, each to his neighbor, and you made a covenant before me in the house that is called by my name.

16 But then you turned around and profaned my name when each of you took back his male and female slaves, whom you had set free according to their desire, and you brought them into subjection to be your slaves.

17 Therefore, thus says YHWH: You have not obeyed me by proclaiming liberty, every one to his brother and to his neighbor. Behold, I proclaim to you liberty to the sword, to pestilence, and to famine, declares YHWH. I will make you a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.

18 And the men who transgressed my covenant and did not keep the terms of the covenant that they made before me, I will make them like the calf that they cut in two and passed between its parts —

19 The officials of Yehudah, the officials of Yerushalayim, the eunuchs, the priests, and all the people of the land who passed between the parts of the calf.

20 And I will give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their lives. Their dead bodies shall be food for the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth.

21 And Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah and his officials I will give into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their lives, into the hand of the army of the king of Babylon which has withdrawn from you.

22 Behold, I will command, declares YHWH, and will bring them back to this city, and they will fight against it and take it and burn it with fire. I will make the cities of Yehudah a desolation without inhabitant.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 34 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant of slave release — verses 8-22 — is the covenant violation that triggers one of the most severe covenant indictments in the book. The slave-owners made a covenant before YHWH in his house to release their Hebrew slaves — then immediately recaptured them when the Babylonian siege temporarily lifted.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 34 in full. Verse 18-19 — I will make them like the calf they cut in two and passed between — is the covenant ceremony of Bereshit 15 inverted: the covenant-cutting ceremony, in which passing between the halves meant accepting the curse of the slaughtered animal if the covenant was broken, is now executed against those who broke the slave-release covenant.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 34. Verse 17 — you have not proclaimed liberty, so I proclaim to you liberty to the sword, to pestilence, and to famine. The covenant irony of divine judgment: the liberty they refused to give to their slaves, YHWH gives to the sword, pestilence, and famine over them. The covenant measure-for-measure principle operates with exact precision.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15-16 — you repented and did what was right — but then you turned around and profaned my name. The covenant act of slave-release was genuine covenant repentance, and YHWH acknowledged it as right — verse 15. The reversal — taking the freed slaves back — is therefore not merely social injustice but covenant profanation of YHWH's name. The covenant community made a public covenant in YHWH's house and then publicly broke it. The covenant name of YHWH was staked on their word. When they broke it, they broke it against YHWH.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 35 The Rekhabites — The Sons of Yonadav — They Have Kept Their Father's Command — You Have Not Listened to Me

1 The word that came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH in the days of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah:

2 Go to the house of the Rekhabites and speak with them and bring them to the house of YHWH, into one of the chambers, and offer them wine to drink.

3 So I took Yaazanyahu the son of Yirmeyahu, son of Khabatzinyahu, and his brothers and all his sons and the whole house of the Rekhabites.

4 I brought them to the house of YHWH, into the chamber of the sons of Khanan the son of Yigdalyahu, the man of Creator, which was near the chamber of the officials, above the chamber of Ma'aseiyahu the son of Shallum, keeper of the threshold.

5 Then I set before the sons of the house of the Rekhabites pitchers full of wine, and cups, and I said to them, Drink wine.

6 But they answered, We will drink no wine, for Yonadav the son of Rekhav, our father, commanded us, You shall not drink wine, neither you nor your sons forever.

7 You shall not build a house. You shall not sow seed. You shall not plant or have a vineyard, but you shall live in tents all your days, that you may live many days in the land where you sojourn.

8 We have obeyed the voice of Yonadav the son of Rekhav, our father, in all that he commanded us, to drink no wine all our days, ourselves, our wives, our sons, or our daughters,

9 And not to build houses to dwell in. We have no vineyard or field or seed,

10 But we have lived in tents and have obeyed and done all that Yonadav our father commanded us.

11 But when Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon came up against the land, we said, Come, and let us go to Yerushalayim for fear of the army of the Chaldeans and the army of the Arameans. So we are living in Yerushalayim.

12 Then the word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu:

13 Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Go and say to the people of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, Will you not receive instruction and listen to my words? declares YHWH.

14 The command that Yonadav the son of Rekhav gave to his sons, to drink no wine, has been kept, and they drink none to this day, for they have obeyed their father's command. I have spoken to you persistently, but you have not listened to me.

15 I have sent to you all my servants the prophets, sending them persistently, saying, Turn now every one of you from his evil way, and amend your deeds, and do not go after other gods to serve them, and then you shall dwell in the land that I gave to you and your fathers. But you did not incline your ear or listen to me.

16 The sons of Yonadav the son of Rekhav have kept the command that their father gave them, but this people has not obeyed me.

17 Therefore, thus says YHWH, the Creator of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I am bringing upon Yehudah and all the inhabitants of Yerushalayim all the disaster that I have pronounced against them, because I have spoken to them and they have not listened, I have called to them and they have not answered.

18 But to the house of the Rekhabites Yirmeyahu said, Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Because you have obeyed the command of Yonadav your father and kept all his precepts and done all that he commanded you,

19 Therefore thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Yonadav the son of Rekhav shall never lack a man to stand before me.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 35 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The Rekhabite narrative is the covenant object lesson that reverses the normal prophetic structure: instead of condemning covenant unfaithfulness, Yirmeyahu commends the Rekhabites' faithfulness to their human father's command as the covenant standard against which Yisra'EL's unfaithfulness to YHWH is measured.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 35 in full. Verse 19 — Yonadav the son of Rekhav shall never lack a man to stand before me — is the covenant reward for sustained obedience spanning generations: the family that maintained covenant fidelity to a human father's command across generations receives the covenant promise that they will always have a representative before YHWH.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 35. Verse 14 — the command that Yonadav gave to his sons has been kept to this day — I have spoken to you persistently, but you have not listened. The covenant contrast could not be sharper: a human command obeyed for generations versus YHWH's covenant command persistently ignored. The Rekhabites shame Yisra'EL by their consistency.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6-10 — the Rekhabites refuse the wine offered in the temple precincts. Their refusal is total, reasoned, and multi-generational. They can articulate exactly why they will not drink — because our father commanded us. The covenant community of Yehudah cannot articulate why they follow Ba'al — only habit and the stubbornness of the evil heart — Chapter 17:9. The Rekhabites are the covenant community YHWH wished Yisra'EL had been.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 36 The Scroll of Yirmeyahu — Barukh Reads It in the Temple — The King Burns the Scroll — Write on Another Scroll

1 In the fourth year of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah, this word came to Yirmeyahu from YHWH:

2 Take a scroll and write on it all the words that I have spoken to you against Yisra'EL and Yehudah and all the nations, from the day I spoke to you, from the days of Yoshiyahu until today.

3 It may be that the house of Yehudah will hear all the disaster that I intend to do to them, so that every one may turn from his evil way, and that I may forgive their iniquity and their sin.

4 Then Yirmeyahu called Barukh the son of Neriyahu, and Barukh wrote on a scroll at the dictation of Yirmeyahu all the words of YHWH that he had spoken to him.

5 And Yirmeyahu ordered Barukh, saying, I am banned from going to the house of YHWH,

6 So you are to go, and on a day of fasting in the hearing of all the people in YHWH's house you shall read the words of YHWH from the scroll that you have written at my dictation. You shall read them also in the hearing of all the people of Yehudah who come out of their cities.

7 It may be that their plea for mercy will come before YHWH, and that every one will turn from his evil way, for great is the anger and wrath that YHWH has pronounced against this people.

8 And Barukh the son of Neriyahu did all that Yirmeyahu the prophet ordered him about reading from the scroll the words of YHWH in YHWH's house.

9 In the fifth year of Yeho'yakim the son of Yoshiyahu, king of Yehudah, in the ninth month, all the people in Yerushalayim and all the people who came from the cities of Yehudah to Yerushalayim proclaimed a fast before YHWH.

10 Then, in the hearing of all the people, Barukh read the words of Yirmeyahu from the scroll, in the house of YHWH, in the chamber of Gemaryahu the son of Shafan the secretary, which was in the upper court, at the entry of the New Gate of YHWH's house.

11 When Mikhayahu the son of Gemaryahu, son of Shafan, heard all the words of YHWH from the scroll,

12 He went down to the king's house, into the secretary's chamber, and all the officials were sitting there: Elishama the secretary, Delayahu the son of Shemayahu, Elnatan the son of Akhbor, Gemaryahu the son of Shafan, Tzidkiyahu the son of Hananyahu, and all the officials.

13 And Mikhayahu told them all the words that he had heard, when Barukh read the scroll in the hearing of the people.

14 Then all the officials sent Yehudi the son of Netanyahu, son of Shelemyahu, son of Khushi, to say to Barukh, Take in your hand the scroll that you read in the hearing of the people, and come. So Barukh the son of Neriyahu took the scroll in his hand and came to them.

15 And they said to him, Sit down and read it to us. So Barukh read it to them.

16 When they heard all the words, they turned one to another in fear. And they said to Barukh, We must report all these words to the king.

17 Then they asked Barukh, Tell us, how did you write all these words? Was it at his dictation?

18 Barukh answered them, He dictated all these words to me, while I wrote them with ink on the scroll.

19 Then the officials said to Barukh, Go and hide, you and Yirmeyahu, and let no one know where you are.

20 So they went into the court to the king, having put the scroll in the chamber of Elishama the secretary, and they reported all the words to the king.

21 Then the king sent Yehudi to get the scroll, and he took it from the chamber of Elishama the secretary. And Yehudi read it to the king and all the officials who stood beside the king.

22 It was the ninth month, and the king was sitting in the winter house, and there was a fire burning in the fire pot before him.

23 As Yehudi read three or four columns, the king would cut them off with a knife and throw them into the fire in the fire pot, until the entire scroll was consumed in the fire that was in the fire pot.

24 Yet neither the king nor any of his servants who heard all these words was afraid, nor did they tear their garments.

25 Even when Elnatan and Delayahu and Gemaryahu urged the king not to burn the scroll, he would not listen to them.

26 And the king commanded Yerakhmeel the king's son and Serayahu the son of Azri'el and Shelemyahu the son of Avde'el to seize Barukh the secretary and Yirmeyahu the prophet, but YHWH hid them.

27 Now after the king had burned the scroll with the words that Barukh wrote at Yirmeyahu's dictation, the word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu:

28 Take another scroll and write on it all the former words that were in the first scroll, which Yeho'yakim the king of Yehudah has burned.

29 And concerning Yeho'yakim king of Yehudah you shall say, Thus says YHWH: You have burned this scroll, saying, Why have you written in it that the king of Babylon will certainly come and destroy this land, and will cut off from it man and beast?

30 Therefore thus says YHWH concerning Yeho'yakim king of Yehudah: He shall have none to sit on the throne of Dawid, and his dead body shall be cast out to the heat by day and the frost by night.

31 And I will punish him and his offspring and his servants for their iniquity. I will bring upon them and upon the inhabitants of Yerushalayim and upon the people of Yehudah all the disaster that I have pronounced against them, but they would not hear.

32 Then Yirmeyahu took another scroll and gave it to Barukh the son of Neriyahu, the secretary, who wrote on it at the dictation of Yirmeyahu all the words of the scroll that Yeho'yakim king of Yehudah had burned in the fire. And many similar words were added to them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves portions of Chapter 36. This chapter is the most important piece of evidence for the composition history of the book of Yirmeyahu. The burning of the scroll by Yeho'yakim and the rewriting of a second, longer scroll — verse 32, many similar words were added — explains why the book exists in two distinct textual traditions: the DSS shorter recension and the Masoretic longer recension may represent stages in the book's own recorded history.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 36 in full. Verse 23 — as Yehudi read three or four columns, the king cut them off and threw them into the fire pot. The covenant image of Yeho'yakim systematically cutting and burning the covenant word as it is read is the visual counterpart to Yoshiyahu tearing his garments when the Torah scroll was found in the temple — Melakhim Bet 22:11. Two kings, two responses to the covenant word: one tears in repentance, one cuts in defiance.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for the core narrative of Chapter 36. Verse 32 — many similar words were added to them — is the transparent editorial note that the second scroll contained more than the first. The covenant word that the king attempted to destroy was not destroyed but expanded. The burning of the scroll produced a longer canon.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23-24 — the king cut the scroll and threw it into the fire — yet neither the king nor his servants was afraid, nor did they tear their garments. The covenant contrast with Yoshiyahu is the interpretive key: Yoshiyahu heard the covenant word and tore his garments — Melakhim Bet 22:19 — and received the covenant promise that he would not see the disaster. Yeho'yakim hears the same covenant word, cuts and burns it, and is unmoved. The covenant response to the word determines the covenant outcome.

THE SIEGE NARRATIVES — YIRMEYAHU IN PRISON — THE FALL — CHAPTERS 37 THROUGH 39

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 37 Tzidkiyahu Sends to Yirmeyahu — The Chaldeans Shall Return — Yirmeyahu Arrested at the Gate — Cast Into the Cistern of the Dungeon

1 Tzidkiyahu the son of Yoshiyahu, whom Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon made king in the land of Yehudah, reigned instead of Coniyahu the son of Yeho'yakim.

2 But neither he nor his servants nor the people of the land listened to the words of YHWH that he spoke through Yirmeyahu the prophet.

3 King Tzidkiyahu sent Yukhal the son of Shelemyahu, and Tzefanyahu the priest, the son of Ma'aseiyahu, to Yirmeyahu the prophet, saying, Please pray for us to YHWH our Creator.

4 Now Yirmeyahu was still going in and out among the people, for he had not yet been put in prison.

5 The army of Pharaoh had come out of Egypt, and when the Chaldeans who were besieging Yerushalayim heard news about them, they withdrew from Yerushalayim.

6 Then the word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu the prophet:

7 Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Thus shall you say to the king of Yehudah who sent you to me to inquire of me: Behold, Pharaoh's army that came to help you is about to return to Egypt, to its own land.

8 And the Chaldeans shall come back and fight against this city. They shall capture it and burn it with fire.

9 Thus says YHWH: Do not deceive yourselves, saying, The Chaldeans will surely go away from us, for they will not go away.

10 For even if you had struck down the whole army of Chaldeans who are fighting against you, and there remained of them only wounded men, every man in his tent, they would rise up and burn this city with fire.

11 Now when the Chaldean army had withdrawn from Yerushalayim at the approach of Pharaoh's army,

12 Yirmeyahu set out from Yerushalayim to go to the land of Binyamin to receive his portion there among the people.

13 When he was at the Benjamin Gate, a sentry there named Yiriyahu the son of Shelemyahu, son of Khananiyahu, seized Yirmeyahu the prophet, saying, You are deserting to the Chaldeans.

14 And Yirmeyahu said, It is a lie — I am not deserting to the Chaldeans. But Yiriyahu would not listen to him, and seized Yirmeyahu and brought him to the officials.

15 And the officials were enraged at Yirmeyahu, and they beat him and imprisoned him in the house of Yehonatan the secretary, for it had been made a prison.

16 When Yirmeyahu had come into the dungeon house and the cells, Yirmeyahu remained there many days.

17 Then King Tzidkiyahu sent for him and received him. The king questioned him secretly in his house and said, Is there any word from YHWH? Yirmeyahu said, There is. Then he said, You shall be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon.

18 Yirmeyahu also said to King Tzidkiyahu, What wrong have I done to you or your servants or this people, that you have put me in prison?

19 Where are your prophets who prophesied to you, saying, The king of Babylon will not come against you and against this land?

20 Now hear, please, O my lord the king: let my humble plea come before you, and do not send me back to the house of Yehonatan the secretary, lest I die there.

21 So King Tzidkiyahu gave orders, and they committed Yirmeyahu to the court of the guard. And a loaf of bread was given him daily from the bakers' street, until all the bread of the city was gone. So Yirmeyahu remained in the court of the guard.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 37 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The narrative of Yirmeyahu's arrest and imprisonment marks the transition to the siege narrative that dominates Chapters 37-39. The accusation of desertion — verse 13 — is the covenant misreading of Yirmeyahu's covenant counsel: his instruction to surrender is interpreted as personal treason rather than covenant prophetic word.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 37 in full. Verse 17 — is there any word from YHWH? Yirmeyahu said, There is. Then he said, You shall be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon. The private covenant inquiry of Tzidkiyahu receives the same covenant answer as the public proclamation. YHWH's covenant word does not change in private audiences.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 37. Verse 19 — where are your prophets who prophesied, The king of Babylon will not come? The covenant accountability question directed at the king: the false prophets who gave the covenant-opposite message are gone. Their word failed. Yirmeyahu's covenant word stands — Babylon has come.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — the king questions him secretly. Tzidkiyahu believes the covenant prophet but cannot publicly acknowledge it. He is a king caught between the covenant word he privately credits and the political reality he cannot escape. His private inquiry of Yirmeyahu throughout Chapters 37-38 reveals a man who knows the truth and cannot act on it. The covenant tragedy of Tzidkiyahu is not defiance but paralysis.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 38 Yirmeyahu Cast Into the Pit — Eved-Melekh the Kushite Rescues Him — The Secret Meeting With Tzidkiyahu — Obey and Live

1 Now Shefatyahu the son of Matan, Gedalyahu the son of Pashkhur, Yukhal the son of Shelemyahu, and Pashkhur the son of Malkiyahu heard the words that Yirmeyahu was saying to all the people,

2 Thus says YHWH: He who stays in this city shall die by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence, but he who goes out to the Chaldeans shall live. He shall have his life as a prize of war, and live.

3 Thus says YHWH: This city shall surely be given into the hand of the army of the king of Babylon and be captured.

4 Then the officials said to the king, Let this man be put to death, for he is weakening the hands of the soldiers who are left in this city, and the hands of all the people, by speaking such words to them. For this man is not seeking the welfare of this people, but their harm.

5 King Tzidkiyahu said, Behold, he is in your hands, for the king can do nothing against you.

6 So they took Yirmeyahu and cast him into the cistern of Malkiyahu, the king's son, which was in the court of the guard, letting Yirmeyahu down by ropes. And there was no water in the cistern, but only mud, and Yirmeyahu sank in the mud.

7 When Eved-Melekh the Kushite, a eunuch who was in the king's house, heard that they had put Yirmeyahu into the cistern — the king was sitting in the Benjamin Gate —

8 Eved-Melekh went from the king's house and said to the king,

9 My lord the king, these men have done evil in all that they did to Yirmeyahu the prophet by casting him into the cistern, and he will die there of hunger, for there is no bread left in the city.

10 Then the king commanded Eved-Melekh the Kushite, Take thirty men with you from here, and lift Yirmeyahu the prophet out of the cistern before he dies.

11 So Eved-Melekh took the men with him and went to the house of the king, to a wardrobe in the storehouse, and took from there old rags and worn-out clothes, which he let down to Yirmeyahu in the cistern by ropes.

12 Then Eved-Melekh the Kushite said to Yirmeyahu, Put the rags and clothes between your armpits and the ropes. Yirmeyahu did so.

13 Then they drew Yirmeyahu up with ropes and lifted him out of the cistern. And Yirmeyahu remained in the court of the guard.

14 King Tzidkiyahu sent for Yirmeyahu the prophet and received him at the third entrance of the temple of YHWH. The king said to Yirmeyahu, I will ask you a question — hide nothing from me.

15 Yirmeyahu said to Tzidkiyahu, If I tell you, will you not surely put me to death? And if I give you counsel, you will not listen to me.

16 Then King Tzidkiyahu swore secretly to Yirmeyahu, As YHWH lives, who made our souls, I will not put you to death or deliver you into the hand of these men who seek your life.

17 Then Yirmeyahu said to Tzidkiyahu, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: If you will surrender to the officials of the king of Babylon, then your life shall be spared, and this city shall not be burned with fire, and you and your house shall live.

18 But if you do not surrender to the officials of the king of Babylon, then this city shall be given into the hand of the Chaldeans, and they shall burn it with fire, and you shall not escape from their hand.

19 King Tzidkiyahu said to Yirmeyahu, I am afraid of the Yehudim who have deserted to the Chaldeans, lest I be handed over to them and they deal cruelly with me.

20 Yirmeyahu said, You shall not be given to them. Obey now the voice of YHWH in what I say to you, and it shall be well with you, and your life shall be spared.

21 But if you refuse to surrender, this is the vision that YHWH has shown to me:

22 Behold, all the women left in the house of the king of Yehudah were being led out to the officials of the king of Babylon and were saying, Your trusted friends have deceived you and prevailed against you. Now that your feet are sunk in the mud, they have turned away from you.

23 All your wives and your sons shall be led out to the Chaldeans, and you yourself shall not escape from their hand, but shall be seized by the king of Babylon, and this city shall be burned with fire.

24 Then Tzidkiyahu said to Yirmeyahu, Let no one know of these words, and you shall not die.

25 If the officials hear that I have spoken with you and come to you and say to you, Tell us what you said to the king and what the king said to you — hide nothing from us and we will not put you to death —

26 Then you shall say to them, I made a humble plea to the king that he would not send me back to the house of Yehonatan to die there.

27 Then all the officials came to Yirmeyahu and asked him, and he answered them as the king had instructed him. So they stopped speaking with him, for the conversation had not been overheard.

28 And Yirmeyahu remained in the court of the guard until the day that Yerushalayim was taken. And he was there when Yerushalayim was taken.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 38 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The rescue of Yirmeyahu by Eved-Melekh the Kushite — a foreigner, a palace eunuch from the land of Kush — is the covenant inversion of the chapter: the covenant people's officials cast the prophet into a mud pit; a Kushite from outside the covenant community saves him. The covenant community fails covenant loyalty while the outsider demonstrates it.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 38 in full. Eved-Melekh the Kushite — whose name means Servant of the King — is specifically identified by his ethnic origin. The Orit tradition preserves the significance of the Kushite identity: this is a man from the Afro-Asiatic corridor, connected to the same cultural geography as the Hebrew people. His courage before the king contrasts absolutely with Tzidkiyahu's paralysis.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 38. Verse 22 — your trusted friends have deceived you — your feet are sunk in the mud. The covenant vision of the women of the palace lamenting over Tzidkiyahu echoes Yirmeyahu's own condition in the mud cistern — verse 6. The king who put the prophet in the mud will himself sink in the mud of his own political choices.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19 — I am afraid of the Yehudim who have deserted to the Chaldeans. Tzidkiyahu's stated reason for not surrendering: fear of fellow covenant people who would mock him as a traitor. The covenant tragedy: the king believes YHWH's word through Yirmeyahu — his private inquiries demonstrate this — but he cannot bring himself to act on it. He fears human judgment more than covenant consequence. The covenant word that could save the city and the king is rejected not from disbelief but from social fear.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 39 The Fall of Yerushalayim — Tzidkiyahu Flees and Is Captured — The City Is Burned — Yirmeyahu Is Released — Eved-Melekh Is Promised Safety

1 In the ninth year of Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah, in the tenth month, Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon and all his army came against Yerushalayim and besieged it.

2 In the eleventh year of Tzidkiyahu, in the fourth month, on the ninth day of the month, a breach was made in the city.

3 Then all the officials of the king of Babylon came and sat in the middle gate: Nergal-Sar-Etzer, Samgar, Nevu-Sar-Sekhim the chief officer, Nergal-Sar-Etzer the chief officer, and all the rest of the officials of the king of Babylon.

4 When Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah and all the soldiers saw them, they fled, going out of the city at night by way of the king's garden through the gate between the two walls, and they went toward the Arabah.

5 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued them and overtook Tzidkiyahu in the plains of Yericho. And when they had taken him, they brought him up to Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon, at Rivlah, in the land of Khamat, and he pronounced judgment on him.

6 The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Tzidkiyahu at Rivlah before his eyes, and the king of Babylon slaughtered all the nobles of Yehudah.

7 He put out the eyes of Tzidkiyahu and bound him in chains to take him to Babylon.

8 The Chaldeans burned the king's house and the house of the people, and broke down the walls of Yerushalayim.

9 Then Nevuzaradan, the captain of the guard, carried into exile to Babylon the rest of the people who were left in the city, those who had deserted to him, and the people who remained.

10 Nevuzaradan, the captain of the guard, left in the land of Yehudah some of the poor people who owned nothing, and gave them vineyards and fields at the same time.

11 Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon gave command concerning Yirmeyahu through Nevuzaradan, the captain of the guard, saying,

12 Take him, look after him well, and do him no harm, but deal with him as he tells you.

13 So Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard, Nevu-Shasban the chief officer, Nergal-Sar-Etzer the chief officer, and all the chief officers of the king of Babylon sent

14 And took Yirmeyahu from the court of the guard. They entrusted him to Gedalyahu the son of Akhikam, son of Shafan, that he should take him home. So he lived among the people.

15 The word of YHWH came to Yirmeyahu while he was shut up in the court of the guard:

16 Go, and say to Eved-Melekh the Kushite, Thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I will fulfill my words against this city for harm and not for good, and they shall be accomplished before you on that day.

17 But I will deliver you on that day, declares YHWH, and you shall not be given into the hand of the men of whom you are afraid.

18 For I will surely save you, and you shall not fall by the sword, but you shall have your life as a prize of war, because you have put your trust in me, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension preserves Chapter 39 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The fall of Yerushalayim — the event toward which the entire book has been building since Chapter 1 — is narrated with stark economy: a breach in the wall, the king fleeing, the princes sitting in the gate, the sons killed before the father's eyes, the father blinded, the city burned. The covenant judgment has arrived exactly as the covenant prophet declared.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 39 in full. Verse 17-18 — I will deliver you — you shall have your life as a prize of war, because you have put your trust in me. The covenant promise to Eved-Melekh the Kushite is the same promise given to those who surrender to Babylon in Chapter 38:2 — life as a prize of war. The outsider who trusted YHWH receives the covenant promise the insiders refused.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 39. Verse 7 — he put out the eyes of Tzidkiyahu and bound him in chains. The last thing Tzidkiyahu sees before the blinding is the deaths of his sons — verse 6. Yirmeyahu's covenant word of Chapter 34:4-5 is partially fulfilled: Tzidkiyahu will not die by the sword. But the covenant hope of dying in peace is forfeit because he did not obey the covenant word.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11-14 — Nevukhadnetzar commands that Yirmeyahu be treated well and given to Gedalyahu. The covenant irony of the fall: Yirmeyahu, imprisoned by his own people for declaring YHWH's covenant judgment, is released and protected by the Babylonians. The covenant prophet is safer in Babylonian custody than in Yehudite imprisonment. Verse 18 — because you have put your trust in me. The covenant reward of Eved-Melekh is the single positive covenant example in the entire siege narrative: one man trusted YHWH's word and acted on it.

END OF PART FIVE — CHAPTERS 32 THROUGH 39
